gaps.title: Tier boundary overflow
gaps.breaks: Requests exceeding tier limits silently route to wrong tier
gaps.scenario: Growth tier customer (5K ticket limit) has 5,001 tickets but system incorrectly routes LIGHT tier requests to MEDIUM tier, consuming 2x resources
gaps.prompt: Write a test that creates a tenant at Growth tier (5K tickets), sets their ticket count to 5,001, then attempts to route a request that should be LIGHT tier but gets incorrectly routed to MEDIUM tier. Verify the wrong tier is used and resource consumption is incorrect.

gaps.title: Race condition in provider failover
gaps.breaks: Concurrent requests during provider failure cause inconsistent routing
gaps.scenario: Google AI Studio provider fails while 10 concurrent requests are being processed - some get stuck in retry loop, others get routed to fallback provider, causing inconsistent responses
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates 10 concurrent requests to the AI routing engine while simulating a Google AI Studio provider failure. Verify that all requests either successfully failover or fail gracefully, with no requests stuck in an infinite retry state.

gaps.title: Variant isolation breach
gaps.breaks: Configuration changes to one variant affect other variants
gaps.scenario: Team updates LIGHT tier variant config to use Groq provider but accidentally affects MEDIUM tier variant, causing all MEDIUM tier requests to use the wrong provider
gaps.prompt: Write a test that creates two variants (LIGHT and MEDIUM tier) with different provider configurations, then modifies only the LIGHT tier variant configuration. Verify that the MEDIUM tier variant configuration remains unchanged and continues to use its original provider.

gaps.title: Silent failure in provider health check
gaps.breaks: Unhealthy providers not properly detected, causing routing failures
gaps.scenario: Cerebras provider has intermittent connectivity issues but health check passes, causing 30% of requests to fail until manually discovered
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates a Cerebras provider with intermittent connectivity issues (random 30% failure rate) while the health check endpoint returns 200 OK. Verify that the routing engine detects these failures and fails over to the backup provider.

gaps.title: Idempotency violation in routing
gaps.breaks: Duplicate requests get processed multiple times
gaps.scenario: Payment webhook double-fires while a request is being routed, causing the same request to be processed twice with different provider responses
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates a payment webhook double-firing scenario during a request routing process. Verify that the duplicate request is detected and not processed twice, maintaining idempotency.

gaps.title: Tenant isolation in variant config
gaps.scenario: Tenant A configures their LIGHT tier variant to use Groq provider, but Tenant B's requests start using this configuration due to cache key collision
gaps.prompt: Write a test that creates two tenants (A and B) with different variant configurations for the same tier. Verify that Tenant B cannot access or be affected by Tenant A's variant configuration, even when both are using the same tier.

raw_response:
GAPS FOUND: 6

GAP 1
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Tier boundary overflow
What breaks: Requests exceeding tier limits silently route to wrong tier
Real scenario: Growth tier customer (5K ticket limit) has 5,001 tickets but system incorrectly routes LIGHT tier requests to MEDIUM tier, consuming 2x resources
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates a tenant at Growth tier (5K tickets), sets their ticket count to 5,001, then attempts to route a request that should be LIGHT tier but gets incorrectly routed to MEDIUM tier. Verify the wrong tier is used and resource consumption is incorrect.

GAP 2
Severity: HIGH
Title: Race condition in provider failover
What breaks: Concurrent requests during provider failure cause inconsistent routing
Real scenario: Google AI Studio provider fails while 10 concurrent requests are being processed - some get stuck in retry loop, others get routed to fallback provider, causing inconsistent responses
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates 10 concurrent requests to the AI routing engine while simulating a Google AI Studio provider failure. Verify that all requests either successfully failover or fail gracefully, with no requests stuck in an infinite retry state.

GAP 3
Severity: HIGH
Title: Variant isolation breach
What breaks: Configuration changes to one variant affect other variants
Real scenario: Team updates LIGHT tier variant config to use Groq provider but accidentally affects MEDIUM tier variant, causing all MEDIUM tier requests to use the wrong provider
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates two variants (LIGHT and MEDIUM tier) with different provider configurations, then modifies only the LIGHT tier variant configuration. Verify that the MEDIUM tier variant configuration remains unchanged and continues to use its original provider.

GAP 4
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Silent failure in provider health check
What breaks: Unhealthy providers not properly detected, causing routing failures
Real scenario: Cerebras provider has intermittent connectivity issues but health check passes, causing 30% of requests to fail until manually discovered
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates a Cerebras provider with intermittent connectivity issues (random 30% failure rate) while the health check endpoint returns 200 OK. Verify that the routing engine detects these failures and fails over to the backup provider.

GAP 5
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Idempotency violation in routing
What breaks: Duplicate requests get processed multiple times
Real scenario: Payment webhook double-fires while a request is being routed, causing the same request to be processed twice with different provider responses
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates a payment webhook double-firing scenario during a request routing process. Verify that the duplicate request is detected and not processed twice, maintaining idempotency.

GAP 6
Severity: HIGH
Title: Tenant isolation in variant config
What breaks: Variant configuration leaks between tenants
Real scenario: Tenant A configures their LIGHT tier variant to use Groq provider, but Tenant B's requests start using this configuration due to cache key collision
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates two tenants (A and B) with different variant configurations for the same tier. Verify that Tenant B cannot access or be affected by Tenant A's variant configuration, even when both are using the same tier.
